metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the plan's `merge_change_409_guard` log event name in the review-bot-ops.md 'signals to watch' spec with the actual implemented marker `MERGE_CHANGE_ERROR` (the greppable journald line prefix emitted by voter.py `_merge_change_error`), and enumerate its sub-event fields (`merge_commit_error`, `merge_files_error`, `mergelist_fetch_error`, `merge_diff_error`) so the runbook grep is correct.
findings.finding: The plan instructs adding a 'signals to watch' entry to review-bot-ops.md for the log event `merge_change_409_guard` (ERROR), but this event name does not exist anywhere in the codebase. The actual greppable journald marker emitted by voter.py when a merge-path REST call fails is `MERGE_CHANGE_ERROR` (with sub-event name field values `merge_commit_error`, `merge_files_error`, `mergelist_fetch_error`, `merge_diff_error`). An agent writing the runbook section using `merge_change_409_guard` would produce a grep command that never matches.
findings.location: What section / infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md target: '"signals to watch" entry in the runbook' and 'merge_change_409_guard (ERROR)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section's runbook bullet, replace `merge_change_409_guard (ERROR)` with `MERGE_CHANGE_ERROR (ERROR — sub-events: merge_commit_error, merge_files_error, mergelist_fetch_error, merge_diff_error)`. The runbook grep should be: `journalctl CONTAINER_NAME=compose-review-bot-1 --no-pager -o cat | grep MERGE_CHANGE_ERROR | tail`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the S4 plan whether 'bot-code rollback = redeploy prior image' belongs as an entry in review-bot-ops.md cross-referencing two-vote-gate-rollback.md as canonical, or as an extension of two-vote-gate-rollback.md (per the epic's run-3 disposition) — avoid authoring an independent rollback entry in review-bot-ops.md that duplicates or contradicts the S6 back-out section.
findings.finding: The plan adds 'bot-code rollback = redeploy prior image' to review-bot-ops.md, but the epic's own run-3 triage comment explicitly flags that 'S6 back-out should EXTEND two-vote-gate-rollback.md (not duplicate).' If S4 writes merge-bot rollback guidance into review-bot-ops.md while S6 later writes the feature-branch back-out into two-vote-gate-rollback.md, operators will face split rollback documentation across two runbooks with no clear canonical reference — a real operational hazard. The S4 plan does not specify how the bot-rollback content divides between the two files or that review-bot-ops.md should cross-reference two-vote-gate-rollback.md as canonical.
findings.location: ## What — 'review-bot-ops.md: merge-change ops … bot-code rollback = redeploy prior image' / AC: 'review-bot-ops.md gains merge-change ops + signals-to-watch + rollback'
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's What section, change the review-bot-ops.md rollback item to: 'bot-code rollback — add a single pointer entry to two-vote-gate-rollback.md §A/§B (the canonical back-out), not a standalone procedure; the S6 story extends two-vote-gate-rollback.md with the full feature-branch back-out.' This keeps rollback documentation in one canonical file and avoids the duplication risk flagged in the epic's run-3 triage.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify for AC3 what concrete content must be present or absent in each file (e.g. 'one-time-setup section references hook install', 'no PR/GitHub merge instructions remain') so the criterion is objectively verifiable.
findings.finding: AC3 ('README + docs/local-dev-env.md + gerrit-aws-setup.md verified/updated') is a 'verify whether' placeholder — it defers the actual content decision to the executor rather than stating what must change.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3; What bullet for README + install docs

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Change AC5 to require the hit-list be recorded in a repo artifact (e.g. a `docs/sweep-notes.md` entry or a comment block in CONTRIBUTING.md) rather than only on the external ticket.
findings.finding: AC5 requires the sweep hit-list to be 'recorded on this ticket' — a location outside repo artifacts — making the evidence non-verifiable from a PR or CI context.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief 'stale-practice sweep log' section to CONTRIBUTING.md or a dedicated `docs/sweep-notes.md` listing each hit and its resolution, making the evidence part of the PR diff.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand AC3 (or AC1) to explicitly include verification/update of the CONTRIBUTING.md one-time-setup section so the described work has a covering criterion.
findings.finding: The 'What' section lists 'CONTRIBUTING one-time setup' as a verification target alongside README.md and docs/local-dev-env.md, but AC3 names only 'README + docs/local-dev-env.md + gerrit-aws-setup.md' — the CONTRIBUTING.md one-time-setup subsection is not explicitly covered by any AC (AC1 covers only the new feature-branch section).
findings.location: What section ('README.md + install docs (docs/local-dev-env.md, CONTRIBUTING one-time setup)') vs AC3 ('README + docs/local-dev-env.md + gerrit-aws-setup.md verified/updated')
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC3 to read: '[ ] README, docs/local-dev-env.md, gerrit-aws-setup.md, and CONTRIBUTING.md one-time-setup section verified/updated for two-vote + feature-branch world.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate in the plan (or in the ticket body) the specific sections/steps of `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` that S1 touched (e.g., vote-label names, submission rules, branch-creation API calls), and make the AC reference that list so 'verified/updated' is falsifiable.
findings.finding: docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md is listed only as 'cross-check after S1 edits' with no enumerated list of what S1 changed or what specific stale references to verify, making the AC 'verified/updated' unverifiable and creating a real risk that S1-introduced changes (new vote labels, updated endpoints, changed setup steps) are silently missed in this file.
findings.location: What section — 'docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md: cross-check after S1 edits'; AC line 'README + docs/local-dev-env.md + gerrit-aws-setup.md verified/updated'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sub-bullet under the gerrit-aws-setup.md entry, e.g.: 'Verify: vote-label names match S1 schema, submission-rule description reflects two-vote requirement, any branch-creation CLI examples match S1 server-side API — record diffs or explicit no-change confirmation on this ticket.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC3 to explicitly include CONTRIBUTING.md §1 (one-time setup) as a verification target, or add a note in the What section clarifying it is covered by the CONTRIBUTING.md feature-branch section AC (AC1) rather than AC3.
findings.finding: The What section lists 'CONTRIBUTING one-time setup' as a verification target, but AC3 names only 'README + docs/local-dev-env.md + gerrit-aws-setup.md' — the CONTRIBUTING.md §1 (one-time setup) is silently dropped from the AC, creating ambiguity about whether that section's hook-install command and two-hook note are in scope for verification.
findings.location: What section ('README.md + install docs (docs/local-dev-env.md, CONTRIBUTING one-time setup)') vs. AC3 ('README + docs/local-dev-env.md + gerrit-aws-setup.md verified/updated')
findings.suggested_fix: Add 'CONTRIBUTING.md §1 (one-time setup)' to AC3: 'README + docs/local-dev-env.md + CONTRIBUTING.md §1 (one-time setup) + gerrit-aws-setup.md verified/updated' — it is already a named target in the What section and its hook-install recipe must cohere with the new feature-branch section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add infra/scripts/observability.sh and infra/terraform/monitoring_88ab.tf as explicit cross-reference sources for the review-bot-ops.md 'signals to watch' entry, so the documented signal names (MERGE_CHANGE_ERROR, merge_change_review, merge_change_409_guard, mergelist_fetch_error, review_bot_merge_change_errors) are verified against the shipped S2 artifacts.
findings.finding: The plan instructs adding a 'signals to watch' entry to review-bot-ops.md for S2 merge-change log events, but does not name infra/scripts/observability.sh or infra/terraform/monitoring_88ab.tf as cross-reference sources. These files define the exact signal names (MERGE_CHANGE_ERROR, merge_change_review, merge_change_409_guard, mergelist_fetch_error, review_bot_merge_change_errors) that the new runbook section must document accurately.
findings.location: What section: 'infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md: merge-change ops — 409 semantics, /rerun on merges, the S2 log signals ("signals to watch"), bot-code rollback = redeploy prior image'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What bullet for review-bot-ops.md, add: 'signal names verified against infra/scripts/observability.sh §4c and infra/terraform/monitoring_88ab.tf alarm resource'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace `<host>` in the hook-install command with the actual Gerrit hostname (or a documented environment variable/config value that the author/agent can look up), so the recipe is copy-pasteable.
findings.finding: The hook-install command in the CONTRIBUTING.md spec contains a literal `<host>` placeholder that an executing agent cannot resolve without external clarification.
findings.location: What bullet: CONTRIBUTING.md, hook-install prerequisite step
findings.suggested_fix: If the hostname is already defined in docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md or an env-var convention, reference it explicitly (e.g. `${GERRIT_HOST}` with a note pointing to where it is set, or hard-code the known URL).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete proving command per AC — e.g. for AC5: `grep -rn 'pull request\|gh pr\|PR merge' CONTRIBUTING.md CLAUDE.md README.md docs/ infra/runbooks/ | grep -v '#.*justified'` must return zero unresolved hits.
findings.finding: No acceptance criterion has a concrete proving command or check; for a documentation plan this is still a gap because the sweep (AC5) and section-completeness (AC1, AC2) claims have no deterministic verification step.
findings.suggested_fix: For AC1/AC2, a section-header grep suffices. For AC5, a literal grep invocation with the exact patterns from the What section makes the sweep reproducible and automatable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC that requires a concrete validation mechanism — e.g., a dry-run walkthrough by a contributor or agent following the new CONTRIBUTING.md section end-to-end (branch creation → story push → catch-up merge → merge-back), or a dogfooding note confirming the hook-install command was verified against the live Gerrit host.
findings.finding: No concrete validation mechanism exists in the ACs — all five acceptance criteria are structural completeness checks ('section complete', 'hit-list recorded', 'zero unjustified hits') rather than a before/after workflow comparison, dogfooding step, or operational signal that a real contributor or agent can successfully execute the feature-branch flow end-to-end using the new docs.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'A contributor (human or agent) performs a dry-run of the full feature-branch flow (branch creation → story loop push → catch-up merge → merge-back) using only the new CONTRIBUTING.md section, and any discovered ambiguities or errors are resolved before merge.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the hook-install command flag form: verify whether `-s` (silent) should be added repo-wide or dropped from the plan's proposed text to match the established `curl -Lo` pattern used in CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md, and github-mirror-lock.md.
findings.finding: The plan specifies `curl -sLo .git/hooks/commit-msg https://<host>/tools/hooks/commit-msg` as the hook-install command to document, but both existing sources (`CONTRIBUTING.md` line 48 and `CLAUDE.md` line 458) use `curl -Lo` (without `-s`). The plan's `-sLo` flag form is inconsistent with the established pattern across all three files that will carry this command.
findings.location: What section — CONTRIBUTING.md hook install prerequisite step
findings.suggested_fix: Change the plan's proposed command to `curl -Lo .git/hooks/commit-msg https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/tools/hooks/commit-msg && chmod +x .git/hooks/commit-msg` to match all three existing call sites.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the CLAUDE.md line-number locator in the plan's What section from `~line 410` to `~line 418` (the actual start of the `## Git workflow` heading).
findings.finding: The plan claims `CLAUDE.md` Git-workflow section is at `~line 410`, but the section heading `## Git workflow (code changes)` actually begins at line 418. This is a minor but concrete locator assertion about the codebase that is verifiably wrong.
findings.suggested_fix: Change `CLAUDE.md Git-workflow section (~line 410)` to `CLAUDE.md Git-workflow section (~line 418)`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit requirement that the new `signals to watch` section in `review-bot-ops.md` uses a heading that exactly matches the anchor text already present in `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` line 300-301 (`"signals to watch"`).
findings.finding: The plan asserts `review-bot-ops.md` will gain a 'signals to watch' section, but `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` (line 300-301) already contains an inbound cross-reference pointing to `infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md` with anchor text 'signals to watch' — a section that does not yet exist in the runbook. The plan's scope-section and AC4 do not explicitly call out resolving this pre-existing dangling cross-reference as a task.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC4: 'heading must be `## Signals to watch` to satisfy the cross-reference already present in gerrit-aws-setup.md line 300.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `GIT_EDITOR=/bin/true git commit --amend` correctly re-runs the `commit-msg` hook and stamps a Change-Id on a merge commit (e.g. validate against the existing test fixtures under `tests/fixtures/review_bot_merge/` or run a live probe), and cite the validation evidence in the plan.
findings.finding: The plan specifies `GIT_EDITOR=/bin/true git commit --amend` as the recipe for stamping a Change-Id on a merge commit, presenting this as an established technique to document. No instance of this command or pattern exists anywhere in the codebase (CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md, runbooks, ADRs). This is an unverified assertion about a technique that will be introduced as new content without any existing code-level or doc-level grounding.
findings.location: What section — CONTRIBUTING.md feature-branch section, Change-Id stamp recipe

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
